description: 'shewo' has two established uses. A true claim or story accords with reality; a true thing is genuine rather than an imitation or pretence, the sense carried by a real stone or the Real rabbit in Phi's literary corpus. 'telua' (accurate) asks how well a representation matches its reference, while 'noemi' (authentic) concerns faithfulness to one's own nature or beliefs.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade towards the postalveolar region and pass unvoiced air for 'sh', then use true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for 'w', continuing into rounded true-mid back 'o'. Stress falls on 'she'.
sound_symbolism: A hush-like opening narrows the airflow before the word rounds through 'w' into 'o'. The compact contour may recall a claim gathered carefully and set down whole.
usage_notes: The adjective covers truth or genuineness, never general intensity or proof that a speaker is honest. Negated 'shewo ma nai' supplies false or not genuine when context makes the contrast clear.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Right Speech gives truthfulness an ethical place while leaving knowledge, intention, kindness, and benefit as further questions.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A truth claim remains open to evidence and correction. 'shewo' assesses the claim rather than granting the speaker moral or social authority.

semantic_domains.cognition: accord between a claim or account and reality
semantic_domains.wisdom: truth or genuineness kept separate from certainty and status
